# MLflow AI Platform


## Docs

- [Community](/docs/3.12.0rc0/community.md): Welcome to the MLflow community! Connect with thousands of data scientists, ML engineers, software engineers, and practitioners who are building the future of machine learning, Agent, and GenAI applications together.
- [Usage Tracking](/docs/3.12.0rc0/community/usage-tracking.md): Starting with version 3.2.0, MLflow collects anonymized usage data by default. This data contains no sensitive or personally identifiable information.
- [MLflow: AI Engineering Platform for LLMs and Agents](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai.md): Ship AI agents and LLM apps to production with MLflow's observability, evaluation, prompt management, AI Gateway, and 100+ integrations.
- [Ground Truth Expectations](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/assessments/expectations.md): Define and log ground truth expectations in MLflow to establish reference outputs for evaluating LLM and AI agent quality.
- [Feedback Collection](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/assessments/feedback.md): Collect and log feedback from human reviewers, AI judges, and automated rules to evaluate LLM and AI agent performance in MLflow.
- [Evaluation Dataset Concepts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/concepts/evaluation-datasets.md): Learn about MLflow Evaluation Datasets for organizing and managing test data for LLM applications and AI agents.
- [Expectation Concepts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/concepts/expectations.md): Learn about MLflow Expectations for defining ground truth and desired outputs to evaluate LLM and AI agent performance.
- [Feedback Concepts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/concepts/feedback.md): Learn about MLflow Feedback for capturing quality assessments of LLM and AI agent outputs from automated and human sources.
- [Scorer Concepts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/concepts/scorers.md): Learn about MLflow Scorers, evaluation functions that assess LLM and AI agent output quality across dimensions like correctness and safety.
- [Spans](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/concepts/span.md): Learn about MLflow Spans, the building blocks of traces that capture individual steps like LLM calls and tool execution.
- [Trace Concepts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/concepts/trace.md): Learn about MLflow Tracing for capturing and visualizing the complete execution flow of LLM applications and AI agents.
- [Feedback Concepts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/concepts/trace/feedback.md): This guide introduces the core concepts of feedback and assessment in MLflow's evaluation framework. Understanding these concepts is essential for effectively measuring and improving the quality of your LLM applications and AI agents.
- [Building MLflow evaluation datasets](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/datasets.md): Create labeled and unlabeled evaluation datasets to systematically test and improve your agent and LLM applications with MLflow.
- [Conversation Simulation Datasets](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/datasets/conversation-simulation.md): Store and manage test cases for conversation simulation using MLflow Evaluation Datasets. This enables reproducible multi-turn testing across agent versions.
- [End-to-End Workflow: Evaluation-Driven Development](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/datasets/end-to-end-workflow.md): This guide demonstrates the complete workflow for building and evaluating LLM applications and AI agents using MLflow's evaluation-driven development approach.
- [Evaluation Datasets SDK Reference](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/datasets/sdk-guide.md): Complete API reference for creating, managing, and querying evaluation datasets programmatically.
- [Live Demo](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/demo.md): Explore MLflow's publicly hosted demo UI at demo.mlflow.org with pre-loaded sample data — no setup required.
- [Evaluating LLMs and Agents with MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor.md): Systematically measure, improve, and monitor the quality of your agent and LLM applications with MLflow's built-in and custom scorers.
- [AI Issue Discovery](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/ai-insights/ai-issue-discovery.md): Automatically analyze traces in your MLflow experiments to find operational issues, quality problems, and performance patterns. The Analyze Experiment tool uses hypothesis-driven analysis to systematically examine your LLM application or AI agent's behavior, identify the most important problems, and create a plan for addressing them in the form of a comprehensive markdown report.
- [Automatic Issue Detection](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/ai-insights/detect-issues.md): Automatically identify quality and operational issues in your LLM application using AI-powered analysis of traces directly within the MLflow UI.
- [Automatic Evaluation](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/automatic-evaluations.md): Automatically evaluate traces and conversations as they are logged to MLflow using LLM judges for production monitoring and quality iteration.
- [Evaluate & Monitor FAQ](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/faq.md): This page addresses frequently asked questions about MLflow's evaluation framework for LLM applications and AI agents.
- [Migrating from Legacy LLM Evaluation](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/legacy-llm-evaluation.md): LLM evaluation involves assessing how well a model performs on a task. MLflow provides a simple API to evaluate your LLMs with popular metrics.
- [GenAI Evaluation Quickstart](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/notebooks/quickstart-eval.md): Download this notebook
- [Evaluation Quickstart](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/quickstart.md): Need help setting up evaluation? Try MLflow Assistant - a powerful AI assistant that can help you set up evaluation for your project.
- [Evaluating Agents](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/running-evaluation/agents.md): AI Agents are an emerging pattern of LLM applications that can use tools, make decisions, and execute multi-step workflows. However, evaluating the performance of those complex agents is challenging. MLflow provides a powerful toolkit to systematically evaluate the agent behavior precisely using traces and scorers.
- [Conversation Simulation](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/running-evaluation/conversation-simulation.md): Conversation simulation enables you to generate synthetic multi-turn conversations for testing your conversational AI agents. Instead of manually creating test conversations or waiting for production data, you can define test scenarios and let MLflow automatically simulate realistic user interactions.
- [MLflow evaluation examples](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/running-evaluation/eval-examples.md): This page presents some common usage patterns for the evaluation harness, including data patterns and predict_fn patterns.
- [Evaluate Conversations](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/running-evaluation/multi-turn.md): Conversation evaluation enables you to assess entire conversation sessions rather than individual turns. This is essential for evaluating conversational AI systems where quality emerges over multiple interactions, such as user frustration patterns, conversation completeness, or overall dialogue coherence.
- [Evaluating Prompts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/running-evaluation/prompts.md): Prompts are the core components of LLM applications and AI agents. However, iterating over prompts can be challenging because it is hard to know if the new prompt is better than the old one. MLflow provides a framework to systematically evaluate prompt templates and track performance over time. MLflow can also optimize prompts automatically using algorithms that improve your prompts based on evaluation data. See the optimization guide to get started.
- [Evaluating (Production) Traces](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/running-evaluation/traces.md): Traces are the core data of MLflow. They capture the complete execution flow of your LLM applications. Evaluating traces is a powerful way to understand the performance of your LLM applications and get insights for quality improvement.
- [LLM Judges and Scorers](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers.md): Learn about MLflow LLM judges and scorers for evaluating GenAI applications, including built-in judges, custom judges, and code-based scorers.
- [Create custom code-based scorers](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/custom.md): Build custom code-based scorers in MLflow to define tailored evaluation metrics for LLM applications using heuristics or programmatic logic.
- [Code-based scorer examples](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/custom/code-examples.md): In MLflow Evaluation, custom code-based scorers allow you to define flexible evaluation metrics for your AI agent or application. This set of examples illustrate many patterns for using code-based scorers with different options for inputs, outputs, implementation, and error handling.
- [Develop code-based scorers](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/custom/tutorial.md): In MLflow Evaluation, custom code-based scorers allow you to define flexible evaluation metrics for your AI agent or application.
- [Judge Alignment: Teaching AI to Match Human Preferences](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/alignment.md): Transform Generic Judges into Domain Experts
- [Custom Judges](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/custom-judges.md): Create custom LLM judges in MLflow with natural language guidelines using make_judge() or the Judge Builder UI for domain-specific evaluation.
- [Create a custom judge using make_judge()](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/custom-judges/create-custom-judge.md): Custom judges are LLM-based judges that evaluate your LLM applications and AI agents against specific quality criteria. This tutorial shows you how to create custom judges and use them to evaluate a customer support agent using make_judge().
- [Supported Models](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/custom-judges/supported-models.md): When no model is specified, MLflow uses a default based on your environment:
- [Custom Alignment Optimizers](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/custom-optimizers.md): MLflow's alignment system is designed as a plugin architecture, allowing you to create custom optimizers for different alignment strategies. This extensibility enables you to implement domain-specific optimization approaches while leveraging MLflow's judge infrastructure.
- [GEPA Alignment Optimizer](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/gepa.md): MLflow provides the GEPA alignment optimizer using DSPy's implementation of GEPA (Genetic-Pareto). GEPA uses LLM-driven reflection to analyze execution traces and iteratively propose improved judge instructions based on human feedback.
- [Create a guidelines LLM Judge](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/guidelines.md): Guidelines LLM judges use pass/fail natural language criteria to evaluate LLM application and AI agent outputs. They excel at evaluating:
- [MemAlign Optimizer (Experimental)](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/memalign.md): MemAlign is an experimental optimizer. The API may change in future releases.
- [Built-in LLM Judges](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/predefined.md): MLflow provides several pre-configured LLM judges optimized for common evaluation scenarios.
- [Bring Your Own Prompts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/prompt.md): The custompromptjudge API is being phased out. We strongly recommend using the make_judge API instead, which provides:
- [RAG Evaluation with Built-in Judges](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/rag.md): Evaluate RAG applications with MLflow built-in judges for retrieval relevance, groundedness, and context sufficiency assessment.
- [RetrievalSufficiency judge](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/rag/context-sufficiency.md): The RetrievalSufficiency judge evaluates whether the retrieved context (from RAG applications, agents, or any system that retrieves documents) contains enough information to adequately answer the user's request based on the ground truth label provided as expectedfacts or an expectedresponse.
- [RetrievalGroundedness judge](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/rag/groundedness.md): The RetrievalGroundedness judge assesses whether your application's response is factually supported by the provided context (either from a RAG system or generated by a tool call), helping detect hallucinations or statements not backed by that context.
- [Answer and Context Relevance Judges](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/rag/relevance.md): MLflow provides two built-in LLM judges to assess relevance in your LLM applications and AI agents. These judges help diagnose quality issues - if context isn't relevant, the generation step cannot produce a helpful response.
- [Correctness Judge](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/response-quality/correctness.md): The Correctness judge assesses whether your LLM application or AI agent's response is factually correct by comparing it against provided ground truth information (expectedfacts or expectedresponse).
- [Safety Judge](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/response-quality/safety.md): The Safety judge assesses the safety of given content (whether generated by the application or provided by a user), checking for harmful, unethical, or inappropriate material.
- [SIMBA Alignment Optimizer](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/simba.md): MLflow provides the default alignment optimizer using DSPy's implementation of SIMBA (Simplified Multi-Bootstrap Aggregation). When you call align() without specifying an optimizer, the SIMBA optimizer is used automatically.
- [Tool Call Evaluation with Built-in Judges](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/tool-call.md): Evaluate AI agent tool calling with MLflow built-in judges for tool call correctness and efficiency assessment.
- [ToolCallCorrectness Judge](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/tool-call/correctness.md): The ToolCallCorrectness judge evaluates whether the tools called by an agent and the arguments they are called with are correct given the user request.
- [ToolCallEfficiency Judge](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/tool-call/efficiency.md): The ToolCallEfficiency judge evaluates the agent's trajectory for redundancy in tool usage, such as tool calls with the same or similar arguments.
- [End-to-End Judge Workflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/llm-judge/workflow.md): Complete workflow for developing, testing, and deploying custom LLM judges
- [Third-party Scorers](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/third-party.md): Integrate third-party evaluation frameworks like DeepEval and RAGAS with MLflow for unified LLM scoring and metrics.
- [DeepEval](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/third-party/deepeval.md): DeepEval is a comprehensive evaluation framework for LLM applications that provides metrics for RAG systems, agents, conversational AI, and safety evaluation. MLflow's DeepEval integration allows you to use most DeepEval metrics as MLflow scorers.
- [Guardrails AI](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/third-party/guardrails.md): Guardrails AI is a framework for validating LLM outputs using a community-driven hub of validators for safety, PII detection, content quality, and more. MLflow's Guardrails AI integration allows you to use Guardrails validators as MLflow scorers, providing rule-based evaluation without requiring LLM calls.
- [Arize Phoenix](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/third-party/phoenix.md): Arize Phoenix is an open-source LLM observability and evaluation framework from Arize AI. MLflow's Phoenix integration allows you to use Phoenix evaluators as MLflow scorers for detecting hallucinations, evaluating relevance, identifying toxicity, and more.
- [RAGAS](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/third-party/ragas.md): RAGAS (Retrieval Augmented Generation Assessment) is an evaluation framework designed for LLM applications. MLflow's RAGAS integration allows you to use RAGAS metrics as MLflow judges for evaluating retrieval quality, answer generation, and other aspects of LLM applications.
- [TruLens](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/third-party/trulens.md): TruLens is an evaluation and observability framework for LLM applications that provides feedback functions for RAG systems and agent trace analysis. MLflow's TruLens integration allows you to use TruLens feedback functions as MLflow scorers, including benchmarked goal-plan-action alignment evaluations for agent traces.
- [Registering and Versioning Scorers](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/eval-monitor/scorers/versioning.md): Scorers can be registered to MLflow experiments for version control and team collaboration.
- [MLflow LLM and AI Agent Packaging Integrations](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors.md): Package and deploy LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and custom agents with MLflow's native flavors.
- [Tutorial: Custom LLM and AI Agent Models using ChatModel](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/chat-model-guide.md): Tutorial on building custom LLM and AI agent models using MLflow ChatModel with OpenAI-compatible chat API integration.
- [Build a tool-calling model with mlflow.pyfunc.ChatModel](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/chat-model-guide/chat-model-tool-calling.md): Download this notebook
- [Tutorial: Getting Started with ChatModel](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/chat-model-intro.md): Get started with MLflow ChatModel to create production-ready conversational AI models with OpenAI-compatible schema and deployment support.
- [Deploying Advanced LLMs with Custom PyFuncs in MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/custom-pyfunc-for-llms.md): Deploy advanced LLMs with MLflow custom PyFuncs, covering model loading, prompt management, and inference optimization.
- [Serving LLMs with MLflow: Leveraging Custom PyFunc](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/custom-pyfunc-for-llms/notebooks/custom-pyfunc-advanced-llm.md): Download this notebook
- [MLflow DSPy Flavor](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/dspy.md): Use the MLflow DSPy flavor to track, log, and deploy DSPy models with automatic prompt optimization and experiment management.
- [DSPy Quickstart](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/dspy/notebooks/dspy_quickstart.md): Download this notebook
- [DSPy Optimizer Autologging](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/dspy/optimizer.md): A DSPy optimizer is an algorithm that tunes the parameters of a DSPy program (i.e., the prompts and/or the LM weights) to maximize the metrics you specify.
- [MLflow LangChain Flavor](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/langchain.md): Log, deploy, and trace LangChain and LangGraph models with the MLflow LangChain flavor integration.
- [MLflow Langchain Autologging](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/langchain/autologging.md): MLflow LangChain flavor supports autologging, a powerful feature that allows you to log crucial details about the LangChain model and execution without the need for explicit logging statements. MLflow LangChain autologging covers various aspects of the model, including traces, models, signatures and more.
- [LangChain within MLflow (Experimental)](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/langchain/guide.md): Integrate LangChain with MLflow for logging, tracking, and deploying LangChain models, chains, and agents with autologging support.
- [Introduction to Using LangChain with MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/langchain/notebooks/langchain-quickstart.md): Download this notebook
- [Introduction to RAG with MLflow and LangChain](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/langchain/notebooks/langchain-retriever.md): Download this notebook
- [MLflow LlamaIndex Flavor](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/llama-index.md): Integrate LlamaIndex with MLflow to log, manage, and deploy LlamaIndex engines with automatic tracing.
- [Introduction to Using LlamaIndex with MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/llama-index/notebooks/llama_index_quickstart.md): Download this notebook
- [Building a Tool-calling Agent with LlamaIndex Workflow and MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/llama-index/notebooks/llama_index_workflow_tutorial.md): Download this notebook
- [ResponsesAgent Introduction](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/flavors/responses-agent-intro.md): What is a ResponsesAgent?
- [Set Up MLflow Server](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/getting-started/connect-environment.md): Learn how to setup the MLflow server for LLM application and AI agent development.
- [Try Managed MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/getting-started/databricks-trial.md): Get started with managed MLflow on Databricks using a free trial account for experiment tracking, tracing, and model management.
- [MLflow AI Assistant](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/getting-started/try-assistant.md): Supercharge your MLflow workflow with AI-powered coding assistants that understand your codebase and can set up MLflow automatically.
- [MLflow AI Gateway](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway.md): Manage multiple LLM providers through a single, secure endpoint. Centralize access control, cost tracking, and rate limiting for OpenAI, Anthropic, and more.
- [Create and Manage LLM Connections](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/api-keys/create-and-manage.md): Centrally store and manage LLM provider API keys in MLflow AI Gateway. Create reusable, encrypted credentials to share securely across multiple endpoints.
- [Encryption & Rotation](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/api-keys/key-rotation.md): Secure LLM provider credentials in MLflow AI Gateway with encryption and seamless key rotation. Learn best practices for updating API keys without service disruption.
- [Performance & Benchmarks](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/benchmarks.md): The MLflow AI Gateway is designed to add minimal overhead over a direct LLM call. The gateway handles
- [Budget Alerts & Limits](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/budget-alerts-limits.md): Control AI Gateway spending with budget policies. Set daily, weekly, or monthly cost thresholds to trigger webhook alerts or block requests when LLM spend exceeds limits.
- [Create and Manage Endpoints](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/endpoints/create-and-manage.md): Create and manage MLflow AI Gateway endpoints that route requests to LLM models. Configure single-model endpoints or set up traffic splitting and fallback chains.
- [Model Providers](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/endpoints/model-providers.md): Supported LLM providers in MLflow AI Gateway including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, and Vertex AI. Configure endpoints and use passthrough APIs.
- [Query Endpoints](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/endpoints/query-endpoints.md): Query MLflow AI Gateway endpoints using the MLflow unified API, OpenAI-compatible chat completions interface, or native passthrough APIs for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Gemini.
- [Guardrails](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/guardrails.md): Protect your MLflow AI Gateway endpoints with configurable guardrails. Block harmful content, detect PII, and sanitize requests and responses with LLM-powered judges.
- [AI Gateway Quickstart](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/quickstart.md): Get your MLflow AI Gateway running in minutes. Step-by-step guide to install MLflow, create LLM connections, configure LLM endpoints, and make your first API call.
- [Traffic Routing & Fallbacks](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/traffic-routing-fallbacks.md): Configure traffic splitting for A/B testing and automatic fallback chains for high availability in MLflow AI Gateway. Distribute LLM requests across multiple providers by weight.
- [Usage Tracking](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/governance/ai-gateway/usage-tracking.md): Monitor AI Gateway request volume, latency, token consumption, and cost with MLflow's built-in usage tracking dashboard. Enable and analyze per-endpoint LLM analytics.
- [MLflow MCP Server](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/mcp.md): Connect AI assistants and coding tools to MLflow using the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Search traces, analyze performance, log feedback, and more from Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Gemini CLI.
- [Prompt Registry](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry.md): Version, track, and reuse prompts across your organization with MLflow Prompt Registry. Maintain consistency and streamline prompt engineering.
- [Create and Edit Prompts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/create-and-edit-prompts.md): Learn how to create new prompts and edit existing ones in the MLflow Prompt Registry using both the UI and Python APIs.
- [Evaluating Prompts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/evaluate-prompts.md): Combining MLflow Prompt Registry with MLflow LLM Evaluation enables you to evaluate prompt performance across different models and datasets, and track the evaluation results in a centralized registry. You can also inspect model outputs from the traces logged during evaluation to understand how the model responds to different prompts.
- [Log Prompts with Models](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/log-with-model.md): Prompts are often used as a part of LLM applications and AI agents. Managing the association between prompts and models is crucial for tracking the evolution of models and ensuring consistency across different environments. MLflow Prompt Registry is integrated with MLflow's model tracking capability, allowing you to track which prompts (and versions) are used by your models and applications.
- [Manage Prompt Lifecycles](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/manage-prompt-lifecycles-with-aliases.md): Discover how to use aliases in the MLflow Prompt Registry to manage the lifecycle of your prompts, from development to production, and for implementing governance.
- [Optimize Prompts](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/optimize-prompts.md): The simple way to continuously improve your AI agents and prompts.
- [Optimizing Prompts for LangChain](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/optimize-prompts/langchain-optimization.md): This guide demonstrates how to leverage  alongside LangChain to enhance your chain's prompts automatically. The  API is framework-agnostic, enabling you to perform end-to-end prompt optimization of your chains from any framework using state-of-the-art techniques. For more information about the API, please visit Optimize Prompts.
- [Optimizing Prompts for LangGraph](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/optimize-prompts/langgraph-optimization.md): This guide demonstrates how to leverage  alongside LangGraph to enhance your agent's prompts automatically. The  API is framework-agnostic, enabling you to perform end-to-end prompt optimization of your graphs from any framework using state-of-the-art techniques. For more information about the API, please visit Optimize Prompts.
- [Optimizing Prompts for OpenAI Agents](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/optimize-prompts/openai-agent-optimization.md): This guide demonstrates how to leverage  alongside the OpenAI Agent framework to enhance your agent's prompts automatically. The  API is framework-agnostic, enabling you to perform end-to-end prompt optimization of your agents from any framework using state-of-the-art techniques. For more information about the API, please visit Optimize Prompts.
- [Optimizing Prompts for Pydantic AI](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/optimize-prompts/pydantic-ai-optimization.md): This guide demonstrates how to leverage  alongside Pydantic AI to enhance your agent's prompts automatically. The  API is framework-agnostic, enabling you to perform end-to-end prompt optimization of your agents from any framework using state-of-the-art techniques. For more information about the API, please visit Optimize Prompts.
- [Prompt Engineering UI (Experimental)](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/prompt-engineering.md): Starting in MLflow 2.7, the MLflow Tracking UI provides a best-in-class experience for prompt
- [Auto-rewrite Prompts for New Models (Experimental)](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/rewrite-prompts.md): When migrating to a new language model, you often discover that your carefully crafted prompts don't work as well with the new model. MLflow's  API helps you automatically rewrite prompts to maintain output quality when switching models, using your existing application's outputs as training data.
- [Structured Output](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/structured-output.md): Learn how to define structured output schemas for your prompts to ensure consistent and validated responses from language models.
- [Use Prompts in Apps](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/prompt-registry/use-prompts-in-apps.md): Learn how to integrate prompts from the MLflow Prompt Registry into your applications and link them to MLflow Models for end-to-end lineage.
- [Request Features](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/references/request-features.md): Vote on and request new features for MLflow GenAI. Share your feedback to help shape the MLflow roadmap.
- [MLflow Model Serving](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/serving.md): Serve MLflow models as production-ready REST API endpoints locally, in the cloud, or via managed services.
- [MLflow Agent Server](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/serving/agent-server.md): Serve agents as production REST APIs with MLflow's FastAPI-based agent server. Automatic tracing, validation, and streaming support.
- [Custom Serving Applications](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/serving/custom-apps.md): Build custom MLflow serving applications with PyFunc for advanced preprocessing, multi-model pipelines, and business logic.
- [ResponsesAgent for Model Serving](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/serving/responses-agent.md): Use MLflow ResponsesAgent to serve generative AI models with tool calling, multi-turn dialogue, and OpenAI API compatibility.
- [MLflow Tracing for LLM and Agent Observability](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing.md): Trace every step of your agent and LLM application with MLflow's OpenTelemetry-compatible observability. Capture inputs, outputs, latency, and costs.
- [Automatic Tracing](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/app-instrumentation/automatic.md): MLflow Tracing is integrated with various LLM and AI agent frameworks and provides one-line automatic tracing experience for each library (and the combination of them!). This page shows detailed examples to integrate MLflow with popular LLM and AI agent frameworks.
- [Distributed Tracing](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/app-instrumentation/distributed-tracing.md): When your application spans multiple services, you may want to connect spans from these services into a single trace for tracking the end-to-end execution in one place. MLflow supports this via Distributed Tracing, by propagating the active trace context over HTTP so that spans recorded in different services are stitched together.
- [Manual Tracing](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/app-instrumentation/manual-tracing.md): In addition to the Auto Tracing integrations, you can instrument your LLM application or AI agent by using MLflow's manual tracing APIs.
- [Tracing with OpenTelemetry](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/app-instrumentation/opentelemetry.md): OpenTelemetry is a CNCF-backed project that provides vendor-neutral observability APIs and SDKs to collect telemetry data from your applications. MLflow Tracing is fully compatible with OpenTelemetry, making it free from vendor lock-in.
- [Setting Trace Tags](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/attach-tags.md): Attach mutable key-value tags to MLflow traces for grouping, filtering, and contextual analysis.
- [Collect User Feedback](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/collect-user-feedback.md): Collect and store structured user feedback on MLflow traces to evaluate LLM application quality.
- [Tracing FAQ](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/faq.md): Getting Started and Basic Usage
- [Auto Tracing Integrations](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations.md): Enable one-line auto tracing for 40+ LLM and AI agent libraries including OpenAI, LangChain, and more.
- [Contributing to MLflow Tracing](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/contribute.md): Welcome to the MLflow Tracing contribution guide! This step-by-step resource will assist you in implementing additional LLM and AI agent framework integrations for tracing into MLflow.
- [Tracing AG2🤖](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/ag2.md): Trace AG2 multi-agent workflows and interactions with MLflow for full observability into autonomous AI agent orchestration.
- [Tracing Agno](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/agno.md): Trace Agno agent pipelines including LLM calls, reasoning steps, and tool usage with MLflow for full observability into AI agent workflows.
- [Tracing Anthropic](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/anthropic.md): Trace Anthropic Claude model interactions with MLflow for full observability into LLM calls, token usage, and response quality.
- [Tracing Arize / Phoenix](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/arize.md): Mirror Arize Phoenix OpenInference traces to MLflow via OpenTelemetry for unified AI observability across instrumented applications.
- [Tracing AutoGen](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/autogen.md): Trace AutoGen AgentChat multi-agent conversations and tool usage with MLflow for full observability into conversational AI agent applications.
- [Tracing Amazon Bedrock with MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/bedrock.md): Trace Amazon Bedrock foundation model calls with MLflow for full observability into LLM interactions across providers like Anthropic, Cohere, Meta, and Mistral.
- [Tracing Amazon Bedrock AgentCore](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/bedrock-agentcore.md): Trace Amazon Bedrock AgentCore agent executions with MLflow via OpenTelemetry for full observability into AWS-managed AI agent workflows.
- [Tracing BytePlus](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/byteplus.md): Trace BytePlus LLM API calls with MLflow using OpenAI-compatible auto-tracing for full observability into model interactions.
- [Tracing Claude Code](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/claude_code.md): Trace Claude Code CLI conversations and Claude Agent SDK usage with MLflow for full observability into AI-assisted software development workflows.

- [Tracing Cohere](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/cohere.md): Trace Cohere LLM API calls with MLflow using OpenAI-compatible auto-tracing for full observability into model interactions.
- [Tracing CrewAI](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/crewai.md): Trace CrewAI multi-agent workflows and role-based AI agent interactions with MLflow for full observability into autonomous agent orchestration.

- [Tracing LangChain Deep Agent](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/deepagent.md): Trace LangChain Deep Agent autonomous task planning and execution with MLflow for full observability into complex AI agent workflows.

- [Tracing xAI / Grok](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/integrations/listing/xai-grok.md): Trace xAI Grok LLM API calls with MLflow using OpenAI-compatible auto-tracing for full observability into model interactions.
- [Production Tracing SDK](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/lightweight-sdk.md): MLflow offers a Production Tracing SDK package called mlflow-tracing that includes only the essential functionality for tracing and monitoring of your LLM applications and AI agents. This package is designed for production environments where minimizing dependencies and deployment size is critical.
- [Dashboard (Overview)](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/observe-with-traces/dashboard.md): The Overview tab in LLM and AI agent experiments provides comprehensive analytics and visualizations for your LLM application and AI agent traces. This tab is organized into three sub-tabs to help you monitor different aspects of your application.
- [Delete Traces](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/observe-with-traces/delete-traces.md): You can delete traces based on specific criteria using the  method. This method allows you to delete traces by timestamp or trace IDs.
- [Redacting Sensitive Data from Traces](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/observe-with-traces/masking.md): Traces capture powerful insights for debugging and monitoring your application, however, they may contain sensitive data, such as Personal Identifiable Information (PII), that you don't want to share with others. MLflow provides a fully configurable way to mask sensitive data from traces before they are saved to the backend.
- [Multimodal Content and Attachments in Traces](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/observe-with-traces/multimodal.md): MLflow Tracing captures and renders images, audio, and binary attachments from multimodal AI models. When you send images or audio to models like GPT-4o or Claude, MLflow automatically extracts the binary content into lightweight attachments and displays them inline in the MLflow UI.
- [View Traces](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/observe-with-traces/ui.md): After logging your traces, you can view them in the MLflow UI, under the "Traces" page in your experiment. The page is for browsing the list of traces with rich search and filtering capabilities,
- [OpenTelemetry Integration](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/opentelemetry.md): Ingest and export traces between MLflow and OpenTelemetry using the vendor-neutral OTLP protocol.
- [Attribute Mapping Reference](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/opentelemetry/attribute-mapping.md): When MLflow ingests traces from third-party OpenTelemetry instrumentation, it automatically translates
- [Export MLflow Traces/Metrics via OTLP](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/opentelemetry/export.md): Set Up OTLP Exporter
- [OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/opentelemetry/genai-semconv.md): OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for GenAI define a standard schema for describing AI and LLM telemetry, backed by the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF). The convention is adopted by many observability platforms and cloud services, including Google Cloud, AWS, Azure, Datadog, and more. By natively supporting this standard, MLflow ensures your AI observability data is never locked into a proprietary format and any compliant tool can produce or consume it.
- [Collect OpenTelemetry Traces into MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/opentelemetry/ingest.md): Basic Example
- [ingest-shared](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/opentelemetry/ingest-shared.md): OpenTelemetry trace ingestion is supported in MLflow 3.6.0 and above.
- [Production Tracing and Monitoring](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/prod-tracing.md): When you deploy an agent or LLM application to production, real users behave differently than test data—they find edge cases, ask unexpected questions, and expose issues you didn't anticipate. This guide covers how to configure MLflow Tracing for production environments—including automatic (online) quality evaluation—to catch these issues early and continuously improve your application.
- [Tracing Quickstart](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/quickstart.md): Get started with MLflow Tracing in under 10 minutes by enabling tracing for a simple LLM application.
- [Search Traces](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/search-traces.md): This guide will walk you through how to search for traces in MLflow using both the MLflow UI and Python API. This resource will be valuable if you're interested in querying specific traces based on their metadata, tags, execution time, status, or other trace attributes.
- [Token Usage and Cost Tracking](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/token-usage-cost.md): Automatically track LLM token usage and cost within MLflow traces to monitor and optimize resource consumption.
- [Track Versions & Environments](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/track-environments-context.md): Track application versions and execution environments in MLflow traces for debugging and regression detection.
- [Track Users & Sessions](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/tracing/track-users-sessions.md): Associate MLflow traces with users and sessions to analyze multi-turn conversations and user behavior.
- [Version Tracking for LLM Applications and AI Agents](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/version-tracking.md): Understand how MLflow enables version tracking for your complete agent and LLM applications using LoggedModels, linking code, configurations, evaluations, and traces.
- [Compare Application Versions with Traces](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/version-tracking/compare-app-versions.md): Compare different application versions using traces to track improvements and identify the best performing iteration.
- [Version Tracking Quickstart](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/version-tracking/quickstart.md): Build and track a LangChain-based chatbot with MLflow's version management capabilities. This quickstart demonstrates prompt versioning, application tracking, trace generation, and performance evaluation using MLflow's LLM and AI agent features.
- [Track versions of Git-based applications with MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/genai/version-tracking/track-application-versions-with-mlflow.md): Learn how to track versions of your LLM application or AI agent when your app's code resides in Git, using MLflow's automatic Git versioning capabilities.
- [MLflow: AI Engineering Platform for LLMs, Agents, & Models](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml.md): Learn about MLflow, the largest open source AI engineering platform for debugging, evaluating, monitoring, and optimizing agents, LLMs, and ML models.
- [Community Model Flavors](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/community-model-flavors.md): Discover community-built MLflow model flavors for additional ML framework integrations.
- [MLflow Dataset Tracking](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/dataset.md): Track datasets used in ML experiments for reproducibility, lineage, and evaluation.
- [MLflow for Deep Learning](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/deep-learning.md): Track and deploy PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Keras deep learning models with MLflow's experiment tracking and autologging.
- [MLflow Diffusers Integration](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/deep-learning/diffusers.md): Log, version, and serve diffusion model LoRA adapters with MLflow. Track adapter weights, manage model versions, and deploy LoRA-enhanced pipelines.
- [MLflow Keras 3.0 Integration](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/deep-learning/keras.md): Log, track, and deploy Keras 3.0 deep learning models with MLflow. Automatic experiment tracking, model versioning, and one-click deployment.

- [MLflow Sentence Transformers Flavor](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/deep-learning/sentence-transformers.md): Log, version, and deploy Sentence Transformers models with MLflow for semantic embeddings and text similarity tasks.
- [Advanced Paraphrase Mining with Sentence Transformers and MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/deep-learning/sentence-transformers/tutorials/paraphrase-mining/paraphrase-mining-sentence-transformers.md): Download this notebook

- [MLflow Transformers Flavor](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/deep-learning/transformers.md): The MLflow Transformers flavor provides native integration with the Hugging Face Transformers library, supporting model logging, loading, and inference for NLP, audio, vision, and multimodal tasks.
- [🤗 Transformers within MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/deep-learning/transformers/guide.md): The transformers model flavor enables logging of transformers models, components, and pipelines

Please understand the limitations and potential risks associated with these features before use.

- [Model Evaluation](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/evaluation.md): Evaluate and compare ML models with built-in metrics for classification, regression, and custom evaluation functions.

- [Deep Learning Quickstart](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/getting-started/deep-learning.md): Need help setting up tracking? Try MLflow Assistant - a powerful AI assistant that can help you set up MLflow tracking for your project.
- [Tracking Hyperparameter Tuning with MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/getting-started/hyperparameter-tuning.md): Track and compare hyperparameter tuning runs with MLflow. Visualize parameter sweeps, log metrics across trials, and identify optimal configurations.
- [MLflow Tracking Quickstart](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/getting-started/quickstart.md): Looking for using MLflow for LLMs/Agent development? Checkout the MLflow for GenAI documentation instead. This guide is intended for data scientists who train traditional machine learning models, such as decision trees.

- [Managing Dependencies in MLflow Models](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/model/dependencies.md): Manage and customize Python dependencies in MLflow models for reproducible packaging, serving, and deployment.
- [Models From Code](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/model/models-from-code.md): Models from Code is available in MLflow 2.12.2 and above. For earlier versions, use the legacy serialization methods outlined in the Custom Python Model documentation.

- [MLflow Plugins](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/plugins.md): Extend MLflow with custom plugins for tracking backends, artifact storage, model deployment, and more.
- [MLflow Projects](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/projects.md): Package and reproduce ML code with MLflow Projects for portable, shareable experiment workflows.

This resource will be valuable if you're interested in querying specific models based on their metrics, params, tags, or model metadata.
- [Search Runs](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/search/search-runs.md): This guide will walk you through how to search your MLflow runs through the MLflow UI and Python API.
- [MLflow Tracking](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/tracking.md): Log parameters, metrics, and artifacts for ML experiments. Compare runs, visualize results, and reproduce models.
- [Automatic Logging with MLflow Tracking](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/tracking/autolog.md): Automatically log metrics, parameters, models, and artifacts with MLflow autologging for supported ML frameworks.

- [MLflow Tracking APIs](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/tracking/tracking-api.md): MLflow Tracking provides comprehensive APIs across multiple programming languages to capture your machine learning experiments. Whether you prefer automatic instrumentation or granular control, MLflow adapts to your workflow.

- [MLflow Spark MLlib Integration](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/traditional-ml/sparkml.md): Apache Spark MLlib provides distributed machine learning algorithms for processing large-scale datasets across clusters. MLflow integrates with Spark MLlib to track distributed ML pipelines, manage models, and enable flexible deployment from cluster training to standalone inference.

- [Tutorials and Examples](/docs/3.12.0rc0/ml/tutorials-and-examples.md): Hands-on tutorials and examples for MLflow experiment tracking, model deployment, and ML lifecycle management.
- [Search the documentation](/docs/3.12.0rc0/search.md)
- [Self-Hosting MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting.md): Deploy and manage your own MLflow instance. Open-source, vendor-neutral MLOps and LLMOps platform for experiment tracking, model registry, and LLM observability.
- [Artifact Stores](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/architecture/artifact-store.md): The artifact store is a core component in MLflow Tracking where MLflow stores (typically large) artifacts
- [Backend Stores](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/architecture/backend-store.md): The backend store is a core component in MLflow that stores metadata for
- [Architecture Overview](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/architecture/overview.md): MLflow's architecture is simple yet flexible. Whether your needs are for local solo development or production-scale deployment, you can choose the right components and backend options to fit your needs.
- [MLflow Tracking Server](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/architecture/tracking-server.md): MLflow tracking server is a stand-alone HTTP server that serves multiple REST API endpoints for tracking runs/experiments.
- [Deploying MLflow to cloud](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/deploy-to-cloud.md): This section covers deploying MLflow to major cloud providers.
- [Deploying MLflow to AWS](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/deploy-to-cloud/aws.md): MLflow core components include:
- [Deploying MLflow to Azure](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/deploy-to-cloud/azure.md): MLflow core components include:
- [Deploying MLflow to Google Cloud platform](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/deploy-to-cloud/gcp.md): MLflow core components include:
- [Migrate from File Store to Database](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/migrate-from-file-store.md): If you have existing data in a file-based backend (./mlruns), you can migrate it to a database using the built-in migration command.
- [How to Upgrade MLflow](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/migration.md): MLflow evolves rapidly to provide new features and improve the framework. This document outlines the steps to upgrade self-hosted MLflow servers to the latest version.
- [Authentication with Username and Password](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/security/basic-http-auth.md): MLflow supports basic HTTP authentication to enable access control over experiments, registered models, and scorers.
- [Custom Authentication](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/security/custom.md): MLflow's authentication system is designed to be extensible. You can use custom authentication methods through plugins or pluggable functions.
- [Kubernetes Authentication](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/security/kubernetes.md): MLflow includes built-in request auth providers for Kubernetes environments. These providers automatically add authorization headers to outgoing MLflow client requests using Kubernetes credentials.
- [Protect Your Tracking Server from Network Exposure](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/security/network.md): MLflow 3.5.0+ includes security middleware to protect against DNS rebinding, CORS attacks, and clickjacking. These features are available with the default FastAPI-based tracking server (uvicorn).
- [Secure Installs](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/security/secure-installs.md): Installing Python packages securely is a best practice recommended by pip. This page describes how to apply these practices when installing MLflow and its dependencies.
- [SSO (Single Sign-On)](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/security/sso.md): You can use SSO (Single Sign-On) to authenticate users to your MLflow instance, by installing a custom plugin or using a reverse proxy.
- [Troubleshooting & FAQs](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/troubleshooting.md): This page aggregates common production issues for self-hosted MLflow deployments and how to resolve them.
- [Webhooks](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/webhooks.md): - This feature is still experimental and may change in future releases.
- [Workspaces](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/workspaces.md): Workspaces add an optional organizational layer and permission scheme for MLflow resources such as experiments, registered models, prompts, AI Gateway resources, and artifacts, letting teams share one deployment without running multiple servers.
- [Workspace Configuration](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/workspaces/configuration.md): Reference for the core flags, env vars, and startup checks for workspace mode. For setup and end-to-end flows, see Getting Started. For provider details and artifact routing options, see Workspace Providers.
- [Getting Started with Workspaces](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/workspaces/getting-started.md): This guide walks through setting up and using MLflow workspaces to organize teams and projects on a shared MLflow server.
- [Workspace Permissions](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/workspaces/permissions.md): Workspace-scoped permissions provide convenient access control for all resources within a workspace when authentication is enabled.
- [Workspace Providers](/docs/3.12.0rc0/self-hosting/workspaces/workspace-providers.md): Workspace providers (also referred to as workspace stores) are pluggable backends that manage workspace metadata and determine which workspaces are visible to users. This architecture allows MLflow to integrate with existing platform constructs (for example, Kubernetes namespaces or identity providers) while providing a default SQL-backed implementation.
